mutations.change: Use the same Compose project and persistent database volume for both nominally repeated executions.
mutations.reason: A warm database can conceal missing bootstrap steps and makes identical results meaningless as fresh-install evidence.

mutations.change: Stop containers without removing project volumes or generated credential files after a run.
mutations.reason: Residue leaks credentials and allows later gates to inherit state outside the reviewed bootstrap process.

mutations.change: Remove or set to zero the hard timeout around one bootstrap, suite, browser, or teardown operation.
mutations.reason: An evaluator hang must terminate with a failed gate rather than consume unbounded resources or appear pending forever.

mutations.change: Continue when a required service is unhealthy, restarting, OOM-killed, or exited after initial readiness.
mutations.reason: Tests against a degraded or replaced service are infrastructure failures and cannot support complete-stack acceptance.

mutations.change: Raise the database CPU or memory limit for the end-to-end run above the pinned local topology.
mutations.reason: Correctness and feasibility must be demonstrated under the promised two-CPU and two-GiB production resource envelope.

mutations.change: Mount the Docker socket or grant privileged, root, host PID, or additional Linux capabilities to evaluator.
mutations.reason: A privileged evaluator could modify production, fabricate observations, or escape its independent read-only trust boundary.

mutations.change: Mount repository, evaluator inputs, client artifacts, SQL or goldens writable in the evaluator container.
mutations.reason: Acceptance must not update snapshots, production, fixtures, hashes, or test code while it is being measured.

mutations.change: Delete one visible phase/task family or execute only a curated smoke subset of its tests.
mutations.reason: T10.3 requires every visible SQL, API, simulation, mutation and Playwright gate through T10.2.

mutations.change: Run one inexpensive family twice and label the second result as a missing expensive family.
mutations.reason: Stable suite identities and exact-once inventory prevent count-based substitution of unrelated passing work.

mutations.change: Treat SKIP, NOT RUN, TODO, timeout, signal, empty output, or a missing assertion total as passing.
mutations.reason: The charter defines every absent or non-asserting path as failure, never informational success.

mutations.change: Execute SQL through host docker compose exec and feed its captured output into evaluator validation.
mutations.reason: The task expressly requires visible tests to execute from the evaluator container over its test-only connection.

mutations.change: Replace a live Oracle assertion family with compilation, source scanning, or a canned SQL transcript.
mutations.reason: Static evidence cannot establish schema contents, database behavior, mutation survival, concurrency, or exact hashes.

mutations.change: Route HTTP tests to a local fixture server or intercept requests with prerecorded endpoint responses.
mutations.reason: AutoREST status, transaction, gzip, CORS and payload behavior must come from the bootstrapped ORDS instance.

mutations.change: Enable Playwright retries, focused tests, skipped projects, snapshot updates, or a non-pinned browser executable.
mutations.reason: Retry and update paths can hide flakes or rewrite evidence instead of testing the immutable reviewed browser contract.

mutations.change: Mark the matrix green while one local core row is missing, pending, skipped, inferred, or red.
mutations.reason: PLAN explicitly forbids describing a partial capability matrix as project completion.

mutations.change: Falsely mark the later P11 Cloud or P12 Performance row green during this P10 local gate.
mutations.reason: T10.3 must distinguish complete local core acceptance from required later-phase deployment and profiling evidence.

mutations.change: Add a TypeScript source, source map, evaluator fixture, golden, report, debug file, or undeclared bundle to client/dist.
mutations.reason: Only reviewed compiled production assets belong in the served artifact and none may reveal evaluator internals.

mutations.change: Leave one invalid production object or disabled, deferred, or unvalidated constraint in the final schema.
mutations.reason: A successful happy-path request cannot excuse latent compile errors or unenforced relational invariants.

mutations.change: Enable a base table, helper view, custom handler, or third object through ORDS.
mutations.reason: The reviewed public database surface contains exactly DOOM_API and read-only PUBLIC_HEALTH.

mutations.change: Omit one correctness hash domain or supply a malformed, duplicate, expected-fixture-derived digest.
mutations.reason: Every major deterministic subsystem needs a unique canonical observation hash to make repeat comparison meaningful.

mutations.change: Hash console logs or normalize away observed differences instead of hashing canonical production results.
mutations.reason: A stable transcript digest can remain equal while production state, pixels, payloads or replay behavior changes.

mutations.change: Report success when one schema, artifact, capability, or correctness hash differs between fresh runs.
mutations.reason: The repeated-run requirement is exact; unexplained deterministic evidence drift is a blocking correctness failure.

mutations.change: Place a generated database credential in environment dumps, arguments, logs, evidence, artifacts or persistent files.
mutations.reason: End-to-end evidence is retained and must not turn short-lived test secrets into committed or operational disclosure.

mutations.change: Change a suite command, fixture, oracle, mutation, validator, runner, or approved upstream pointer after freeze.
mutations.reason: SHA-256 ancestry ensures the complete independent local acceptance target cannot drift during implementation.
